behaviors.description: Required patient fields are validated before downstream processing, and missing fields are all reported.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is validator→orchestrator handoff: the behavior requires both correct missing-field aggregation and preventing downstream calls. Unit was rejected because text says "before any downstream processing," which is cross-module gating, not just one function.
behaviors.test_description: Run intake orchestrator with missing `name` and `insurance ID`; assert error lists both fields and assert insurance/triage/scheduling modules are not invoked.

behaviors.description: Date of birth validation rejects future dates, implausibly old dates, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: This is pure input-validation logic on one field with no module interaction required. Higher levels were rejected because behavior text is strictly about DOB rule evaluation, not orchestration or I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven tests for DOB parser/validator covering future date, >150 years old, invalid string, and valid boundary dates.

behaviors.description: Insurance eligibility check sends correct patient ID/plan code and maps payer statuses.
behaviors.justification: Needs service+API-client wiring to verify outbound payload mapping and response-state translation (`eligible`/`not eligible`/`pending`). System was considered, but behavior does not require real network faults/protocol quirks; it focuses on request-field and status mapping semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Use an in-process fake payer endpoint/client adapter to capture outgoing request and return each status; assert mapped internal eligibility state is correct.

behaviors.description: If payer API is unreachable, eligibility becomes `pending` and intake continues.
behaviors.justification: Failure is in error propagation across modules (payer client error → eligibility state → orchestration continuation). System rejected because behavior is about handling an unreachable condition, which can be reproduced by deterministic client failure injection without real network.
behaviors.test_description: Make payer adapter throw connection/unavailable error; assert eligibility recorded as `pending` and pipeline proceeds to triage/scheduling path.

behaviors.description: Triage invocation payload includes symptoms and configured emergency rubric.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic plumbing around the LLM call, not model-judgment quality. Demotion below Agentic is justified by behavior text: "includes ... in the model input payload" (payload construction) and no requirement about correctness of model output content.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke triage service with known symptoms/rubric config and a stub model client that records request body; assert both are present in final payload.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage marks emergency symptom combinations as high urgency.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model judgment for symptom interpretation (classification quality), which stubs cannot validate. Workflow rejected because this behavior is a single model decision, not multiple agentic steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated live triage calls on emergency symptom sets (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath) and assert high-urgency rate exceeds acceptance threshold.

behaviors.description: Triage result parser persists a clinician-readable explanation field, not only numeric score.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at parser/storage boundary: explanation may be parsed but dropped before persistence. Unit rejected because behavior says "stores" (cross-module persistence effect), not just parse function return value.
behaviors.test_description: Feed triage response containing score + explanation through parser and persistence path; assert stored record includes explanation text field.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage explanation is case-specific and includes symptom-based rationale, not boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly evaluates generated explanation quality/content specificity from live model output. Integration with canned outputs would always pass and hide real generation-quality failures.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live triage on varied symptom cases; score explanations for mention of case symptoms and explicit urgency rationale; fail if outputs are generic boilerplate.

behaviors.description: Scheduler picks first available slot consistent with urgency SLA (same-day high, <=48h medium).
behaviors.justification: Requires scheduler logic + calendar data interaction to verify ordering and SLA windows against real stored availability. Unit rejected because behavior depends on first-available selection over shared schedule state, not isolated pure function logic.
behaviors.test_description: Seed local calendar DB with ordered slots; run scheduling for high and medium urgencies; assert chosen slot is earliest valid one within required window.

behaviors.description: Concurrent patient processing must not double-book a slot.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is race/transaction behavior across scheduler+DB modules under concurrency; local DB integration is the minimum boundary. System considered, but behavior text centers on shared calendar DB consistency, not OS/network/subprocess-specific effects.
behaviors.test_description: Start two concurrent scheduling transactions targeting same free slot in local test DB; assert only one booking succeeds and final state has no duplicate reservation.

behaviors.description: Confirmation includes required fields and visit-type-appropriate preparation instructions.
behaviors.justification: Primarily message composition/template-selection logic from provided inputs. Integration rejected because behavior does not require transport delivery or external dependencies, only content assembly correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test confirmation builder with fixtures for visit types; assert output contains patient name, appointment time, provider, and correct prep instructions.

behaviors.description: Pipeline orchestration completes with deterministic stubbed triage output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly scopes to orchestration with "deterministic stubbed triage output," which disqualifies Agentic dependence on real model judgments. Needs multi-module state handoff through validate→insurance→triage→schedule→confirm.
behaviors.test_description: Run full pipeline in-process with stubbed triage and payer responses; assert each stage executes in order and final confirmation artifact is produced.

behaviors.description: Full live intake pipeline completes end-to-end for a real submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Execution path includes live AI triage call; by rule, real model interaction is required even if final assertion is completion. Workflow was considered but rejected: behavior describes one live agentic step within a broader pipeline, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a realistic intake through production-like environment using live payer API integration and live triage model; assert pipeline auto-completes through confirmation with no manual fallback.
